conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have a child or children that you're responsible for? If yes, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child I'm responsible for. My son Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To properly assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand the rules first.

Let me read the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have the official rules. Let me ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility for Alex.

Does Alex currently live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful information.

Are you currently the person responsible for Alex, and is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year? (This won't affect your eligibility, but it may mean you have to pay a tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us has an income over £60,000.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- Neither you nor your partner has an income over £60,000

Let me provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the necessary criteria:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Since neither you nor your partner has an income over £60,000, you won't have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge either.

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit for Alex if you haven't already done so. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

circumstances: Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Therefore, you meet all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the basic eligibility criteria: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming for him. Additionally, neither the claimant nor their partner has an income over £60,000, so they will not be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
